comments.body:
Quick walked through, some comments, will look deeper:
- For The “yarn app -status“ command
-- it also needs to support take in service name ?
-- For non-service app like MR job, the “Service Status :" should not be printed ?
- The “yarn app -launch” command takes the file location or example-service-type as the param, we also need to support take service name ? This is especially needed for the pre-bundled example service, as user is not expected to manually edit the example json file. 
- may be create a appName option for it ?
- "HDFS patch to JSON service" : typo, should be path

comments.body:
Quick walked through, some comments, will look deeper:
- For The “yarn app -status“ command
-- it also needs to support take in service name ?
-- For non-service app like MR job, the “Service Status :" should not be printed ?
- The “yarn app -launch” command takes the file location or example-service-type as the param, we also need to support take service name ? This is especially needed for the pre-bundled example service, as user is not expected to manually edit the example json file. 
- may be create a appName option for it ?
- "HDFS patch to JSON service" : typo, should be path
